dry: adjective. Free from or lacking moisture.

ray: noun. A beam of light or radiation.

rod: noun. A straight, round stick, shaft, bar, cane, or staff.
rya: noun. A Scandinavian knotted-pile woollen rug, used as bed-cover and tapestry.

sard: noun. A variety of carnelian, of a rich reddish yellow or brownish red color.

sarod: noun. A fretless string instrument used mainly in Indian classical music.
savior: noun. A person who saves someone, rescues another from harm.

savoy: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Savoy (“type of cabbage”).
sir: noun. A man of a higher rank or position.
soar: verb. To fly high with little effort, like a bird.
